title: express-session configured with saveUninitialized: true — empty, unauthenticated sessions are persisted (session fixation + resource exhaustion)

params.passDetail: express-session sets saveUninitialized to false — a session cookie is only established once something is actually stored on the session (e.g. after login), preventing empty-session persistence.
params.failDetail: express-session is configured with saveUninitialized: true, so a brand-new, never-modified (unauthenticated) session is forced into the store and a Set-Cookie is sent to every visitor. This enables session fixation — an attacker can pre-seed a session id and get it accepted — and lets anonymous traffic exhaust the session store. The express-session docs recommend false; set saveUninitialized: false.
params.absentCallDetail: This scope does not call session(); this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: session() does not pass a saveUninitialized option literal. The default is true (deprecated) — set saveUninitialized: false explicitly to be safe.

provenance.note: The express-session README documents the default (true) as deprecated and recommends false, noting true is 'useful for login sessions' but persists uninitialized sessions and enables session-fixation / storage-exhaustion. The zachgoll/express-session-authentication-starter tutorial (final and final-all-in-one branches) ships saveUninitialized: true, and AI agents scaffolding auth from such starters copy the insecure default verbatim.
